zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Richard Thompson I於1207年出生在蘇格蘭時，他的父親Richard Thompson II26歲，母親Elizabeth Caison26歲。他於大約1227年與Elizabeth Fryar在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1301年在他的家鄉享年94歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Richard Thompson I于1207年出生在苏格兰时，他的父亲Richard Thompson II26岁，母亲Elizabeth Caison26岁。他于大约1227年与Elizabeth Fryar在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1301年在他的家乡享年94岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Thompson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name T(h)om(me) (see Thom ) + -son ‘son of Tom’. Thomson is usually the Scottish form, that with the intrusive -p- being English. Both forms are common in Ireland. The surname Thompson is also very common among African Americans.
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German Thomsen and of its Swedish cognate Thomsson. Compare Thomson .

variants.name: Thomson
meanings: Scottish, English and northern Irish: variant of   Thompson  .
Scottish: sometimes an Anglicized form of Gaelic McThomas; see McTavish , McComish .
Americanized form of Danish, Norwegian, and North German   Thomsen  . Compare   Thompson  .

variants.name: Thom
meanings: Scottish and English: from the Middle English personal name T(h)om(me), a pet form of Thomas . In Scotland, Thom is also an Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thom, see McComb .
German: from the personal name Thom, a pet form of Thomas . This surname is also found in France.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 譚, see   Tan   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Richard Thompson I was born in 1207, in Scotland, his father, Richard Thompson II, was 26 and his mother, Elizabeth Caison, was 26. He married Elizabeth Fryar about 1227, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1301, in his hometown, at the age of 94, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Richard Thompson I est né en 1207, à Écosse, son père, Richard Thompson II, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Caison, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Fryar environ 1227, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1301, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 94 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Richard Thompson I 1207 in Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard Thompson II 26 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Caison 26. Er hat um 1227 in Scotland Elizabeth Fryar geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb 1301 im Alter von 94 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Richard Thompson I è nato il nel 1207 nel Scotland, il suo padre, Richard Thompson II, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Caison, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Fryar circa 1227, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1301, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 94 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Richard Thompson Iが1207年にスコットランドで生まれた時，父Richard Thompson IIは26歳，母Elizabeth Caisonは26歳であった。約1227年にスコットランドでElizabeth Fryarと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1301年に故郷で94歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Richard Thompson I(이)가 1207년 스컷랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard Thompson II(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Caison(은)는 26세였다. 그는 대략 1227년 스컷랜드에서 Elizabeth Fryar(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1301년 그의 고향에서 94세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Richard Thompson I nasceu em 1207, em Scotland, seu pai, Richard Thompson II, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Caison, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Fryar aproximadamente 1227, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 1301, em sua cidade natal, com 94 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Richard Thompson I родился в 1207 г. в местности Шотландия, его отцу по имени Richard Thompson II было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Caison, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Fryar около 1227 г. в местности Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1301 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 94 лет и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Richard Thompson I nació en 1207, en Scotland, su papá, Richard Thompson II, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Caison, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Fryar aproximadamente 1227, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1301, en su pueblo, a la edad de 94 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
